race.name: Tour de Fukushima
race.tagline: UCI Gran Fondo qualifier through Fukushima's recovery zones

vitals.location_badge: Naraha Town, Tomioka Town, Fukushima, Japan

terrain.overview: Moderate with rolling short steep hills and sustained climbs along Pacific coastline and rugged inland highlands

logistics.explanation: Remote Japanese coastal location with uncertain airport access but registration already open

length.explanation: Three solid distance options up to 140km covering gran fondo and medio fondo formats

technicality.explanation: Rolling terrain with short steep hills but no extreme technical demands noted

elevation.explanation: Moderate ~2000m elevation across coastal/highland mix but unconfirmed figures

climate.explanation: Pleasant mild June temperatures ideal for coastal riding

altitude.explanation: Low coastal/highland elevations, no high mountain passes

adventure.explanation: Riding through recent nuclear evacuation zones offers unique emotional adventure

prestige.explanation: Franchise UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier for Japanese riders

race_quality.explanation: Two-day format with time trial and granfondo, successful after early editions

experience.explanation: Cultural recovery story combined with stunning coastal scenery

community.explanation: Strong local revival mission builds community purpose

field_depth.explanation: Domestic focus as Japanese qualifier limits international field depth

value.explanation: UCI qualifier status offers qualification value despite unknown costs

expenses.explanation: Japan travel likely expensive for international participants

cultural_impact.explanation: Routes through 2011 disaster zones powerfully showcase Fukushima recovery

biased_opinion.overview: Unique blend of competitive UCI gran fondo racing with profound cultural recovery narrative through recently decontaminated evacuation zones. Must-do for Japan-based riders seeking Niseko Worlds qualification.

logistics.overview: Fukushima Airport (FKS) ~1hr from Hamadori region. Registration opened Feb 1, 2026 via fukushima-cycle-series.jp. Two-day event format TBD.

history.overview: Launched 2023 as Tool de Fukushima (fourth edition 2026). Part of Fukushima Revival Cycle Series promoting recovery 12+ years after 2011 Great East Japan Earthquake and nuclear disaster. Became UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier for 2026 Niseko Worlds.

course_description.character: Two-day format with **time trial**, **granfondo (140km)**, and **mediofondo (80km)** options through Hamadori coastal region. Routes traverse Pacific coastline, rugged inland highlands, and recently de-lifted evacuation zones (Namie/Odaka areas). **Suffering zones**: sustained climbs in coastal headwinds through disaster-affected terrain.

non_negotiables: UCI home qualifier for Japanese Niseko Worlds entry
Routes through 20km nuclear evacuation zones now reopened
